title: Football, Feminism and You

jokes.text: dwarves hate being called midgets, and midgets are small
jokes.setup: Troy explains his memory technique for astronomy
jokes.punchline: Connecting dwarves/midgets to white dwarf stars through offensive terminology
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's naive but earnest approach to learning, though relies on somewhat problematic terminology for the humor

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what I do to remember the stars? I connect them to things I know. Like, white dwarfs are small stars, right? So I think of dwarves. Or midgets.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's offensive.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: It works though!

jokes.text: a movie of the week
jokes.setup: Asked what a star turns into after it collapses
jokes.punchline: Troy gives a completely wrong but culturally logical answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Troy's pop culture brain makes logical sense from his perspective. Excellent economy and misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: A black hole? No wait... a really angry sun.

jokes.text: just like my weiner
jokes.setup: Discussion of a black hole's incredible density
jokes.punchline: Pierce inappropriately compares it to his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce - inappropriate sexual reference that's predictable but fits his character perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: A black hole is so dense, not even light can escape its gravitational pull.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Kind of like my pants after Thanksgiving.

jokes.text: you said weiner. that's funny
jokes.setup: Pierce's inappropriate comment
jokes.punchline: Troy's childlike appreciation of the word itself
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent reaction to Pierce's innuendo shows his childlike nature perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Ha! He said 'duty'.

jokes.text: pierce and troy didn't get along at first, but now they're bonding through mutual adolescence
jokes.setup: Pierce and Troy acting immature together
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dry observation about their regression
jokes.explanation: Sharp, witty observation that captures both characters perfectly. 'Mutual adolescence' is a brilliant phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know, it's fascinating watching you two regress. It's like witnessing evolution in reverse.

jokes.text: shut up, boo-boo head / will you shut up, fart head?
jokes.setup: Jeff's comment about their adolescence
jokes.punchline: They immediately prove his point with childish insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - they immediately prove Jeff's point about mutual adolescence

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh, that's rich coming from you!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, you're one to talk, you old fossil!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Fossil? At least I don't need a nightlight to sleep!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I do NOT need a nightlight!

jokes.text: there is just one of every kind of you, isn't there?
jokes.setup: Dean observes the diverse study group
jokes.punchline: His awkwardly clinical way of noting diversity
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean awkwardness - trying to celebrate diversity in the most uncomfortable way possible

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I've been observing your little study group, and I have to say, it's a beautiful tapestry of diversity. We have a black student, an Asian student, a woman, an old man, a young man, and... well, I'm not entirely sure what you are, but you're definitely something.

jokes.text: well, we are missing a pipsqueak, but we don't want one, so beat it
jokes.setup: Dean commenting on their diversity
jokes.punchline: Pierce rudely dismissing him not knowing he's the Dean
jokes.explanation: Pierce's obliviousness creates perfect cringe moment when he insults the Dean

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: You know, I've always been proud of Greendale's commitment to diversity.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, I don't care what you think, buddy. Now get out of my way.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I'm the Dean.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I don't care if you're the King of England!

jokes.text: sometimes i just roll around campus like a little football
jokes.setup: Dean trying to recover from Pierce's insult
jokes.punchline: His bizarre metaphor for his campus wandering
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird Dean metaphor that perfectly captures his awkward energy

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I'm like a majestic eagle, soaring through the halls of Greendale, surveying my domain from on high. Except the eagle is me, the domain is a community college, and I'm actually just walking around looking for Pierce to tell him his comment really hurt my feelings.

jokes.text: did you know they had a football?
jokes.setup: Dean asking if Troy knew Greendale has a football team
jokes.punchline: Troy's confusion about the concept of having 'a football' (singular)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - literal interpretation reveals the poverty of the program

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Did you know Greendale has a football team?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: A football? Like, just one?

jokes.text: the team's name is the human beings? / yeah. my idea
jokes.setup: Discussion of the football team
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous team name and Dean's pride in it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - a team name so generic it becomes hilarious. Sets up recurring gag.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So what's the name of our football team anyway?

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: The Greendale Human Beings.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: The Human Beings? That's the worst team name I've ever heard.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: It's inclusive! And I'm very proud of it.

jokes.text: well, a lot of these students have been called animals their whole lives
jokes.setup: Explaining why he changed from 'Grizzlies' to 'Human Beings'
jokes.punchline: Dean's accidentally insulting rationale
jokes.explanation: Dean's well-meaning but tone-deaf reasoning reveals his own prejudices

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Well, we realized 'Grizzlies' was sending the wrong message. It implies we're something we're not. We're not actually bears. We're human beings.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: So the new mascot better represents who we are as a school — regular, ordinary human beings. Nothing special about us. Just... humans.

jokes.text: oh, i can help you with that, your honor. i have a wealth of experience in image management, icon development, and y2k preparedness
jokes.setup: Dean mentions mascot problems
jokes.punchline: Pierce's bizarre qualifications including Y2K preparedness
jokes.explanation: Pierce's random expertise list is perfectly in character - Y2K preparedness is beautifully dated

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, I've got experience. I was a mascot back in '73. Plus, I'm Y2K compliant. I've got a bunker, freeze-dried food, and I know how to hot-wire a car.

jokes.text: whoa! yoko ono much?
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to speak for Troy about football
jokes.punchline: Pierce comparing Jeff to Yoko Ono breaking up the Beatles
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that fits Pierce's age and tendency toward inappropriate comparisons

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Troy loves football, he's been talking about it since we got here.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know what? You're like Yoko Ono.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You're breaking up the group. Just like Yoko broke up the Beatles by trying to speak for John.

jokes.text: bros before hoes, troy
jokes.setup: Pierce encouraging Troy to play football
jokes.punchline: Pierce using juvenile slang inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Pierce trying to be cool with outdated slang - character-appropriate but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You should play football, Troy. You've got the build for it. And the speed. You'd be like a beautiful Black gazelle.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Thanks, Pierce.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's totally tight, homie. You'd be all up in there, gettin' jiggy with it on the field.

jokes.text: i wouldn't play for this school with a 10-foot pole
jokes.setup: Troy expressing his disdain for the school's football team
jokes.punchline: Mixing up 'wouldn't touch with a 10-foot pole' with playing football
jokes.explanation: Troy's malapropism works perfectly for his character while making literal sense in football context

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I wouldn't touch that football team with a 10-foot pole.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You literally play football.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I know what I said.

jokes.text: dean, i'm sure troy will sign up for football if and when some accident damages the part of his brain that feels pride
jokes.setup: Dean persisting about Troy playing football
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cutting assessment of what it would take
jokes.explanation: Sharp Jeff wit with medical precision - perfectly captures his lawyer background and protective instincts

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what it would take to get Troy to play football? A football scholarship, a time machine, and a complete personality transplant. Basically, everything that makes Troy, Troy would have to stop existing.

jokes.text: wednesdays and sundays it's used as parking overflow for the mega-church
jokes.setup: Dean saying the field is 'always open' figuratively
jokes.punchline: The pathetic reality of their field usage
jokes.explanation: Reveals the school's poverty and lack of resources in a perfectly Dean-like overshare

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The field is always open figuratively.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: But the pathetic reality of their field usage is that it's never actually used.

jokes.text: oh, no, i don't have to go
jokes.setup: Annie asking if Britta wants to go to the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Britta completely missing the social convention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - missing obvious social cues while trying to be independent

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Do you want to go to the bathroom?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I don't have to go to the bathroom.

jokes.text: girls go in groups. did you learn nothing from stand-up comedy in the '90s?
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why Annie asked Britta to come
jokes.punchline: Reference to hackneyed '90s stand-up comedy tropes
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy about comedy itself - Jeff showing his cultural awareness and disdain for hack material

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie asked Britta to come because... well, you know, it's like that '90s stand-up thing where the comedian goes, 'What's the deal with airline food?' Except instead of that, it's 'What's the deal with my life being a complete mess?'

jokes.text: well, then you should know that nothing says 'i'm a woman' like doing it with me
jokes.setup: Jeff suggesting Britta try more feminine activities
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inappropriate sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce inappropriate comment - fits character but is predictable and crude

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta, maybe you should try some more feminine activities.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, you could start by letting me teach you about being a woman.

jokes.text: will they or won't they? sexual tension
jokes.setup: Jeff and Britta's banter
jokes.punchline: Abed analyzing them like TV characters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary - analyzing TV tropes while being in one. Economy and character integration are flawless.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You guys are like a procedural drama. You have chemistry, unresolved tension, and you'll never actually get together because the network's afraid it'll kill the ratings.

jokes.text: abed, it makes the group uncomfortable when you talk about us like we're characters in a show you're watching
jokes.setup: Abed's meta-commentary
jokes.punchline: Jeff complaining about being treated like a TV character while literally being one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the nature of the show itself - multiple layers of irony work perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You know what's weird about our group? We're basically characters in a sitcom.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? We're real people living real lives.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Are we though? Think about it. We always hang out in the same places, we have quirky traits, and somehow everything resolves in 22 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's insane. I am not a character. I'm a fully realized human being with depth and complexity.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Sure, Jeff. The guy who constantly makes pop culture references and never actually grows as a person.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I don't appreciate being reduced to a trope. This is exactly the kind of reductive thinking that ruins TV!

jokes.text: well, that's sort of my gimmick. but we did lean on that pretty hard last week. i can lay low for an episode
jokes.setup: Jeff's complaint about Abed's meta-commentary
jokes.punchline: Abed acknowledging the show's writing patterns and promising to dial it back
jokes.explanation: Next-level meta-humor - character discussing the show's writing patterns within the show. Perfectly executed Community-style joke.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Abed, your constant meta-commentary is getting out of hand. We get it—you know we're in a TV show.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're right. I apologize. I'll stop pointing out narrative conventions, foreshadowing, and how our adventures always resolve in 22 minutes. I promise to dial it back.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Although, statistically speaking, by next week I'll have forgotten this conversation entirely due to the show's reset button.

jokes.text: most valued customer. henderson trophy cases
jokes.setup: Looking at what appears to be a trophy case
jokes.punchline: The 'trophy' is just a customer appreciation award from a trophy store
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing the school's pathetic attempts at prestige

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Looking at what appears to be a trophy case, the 'trophy' is just a customer appreciation award from a trophy store.

jokes.text: or pancakes!
jokes.setup: Discussion of astronomy-themed snacks (Milky Ways, Mars bars)
jokes.punchline: Troy's completely unrelated food suggestion
jokes.explanation: Troy's complete disconnect from the astronomy theme is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What about a Snickers bar? I mean, it's got peanuts, so it's basically a protein bar. That's space-appropriate.

jokes.text: i'm jeff. and i'm a student at greendale
jokes.setup: Jeff seeing promotional materials featuring himself
jokes.punchline: His horror at being publicly associated with the school
jokes.explanation: Jeff's vanity and embarrassment about Greendale perfectly captured in this moment

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God, no. No, no, no, no, no.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: They put my face on these? On the school? Is this... is this permanent?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This is a nightmare. A actual nightmare. I can't be associated with Greendale like this.

jokes.text: that's mine. my bad
jokes.setup: Teacher complaining about students' cell phones
jokes.punchline: Jeff's phone goes off, revealing his hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reversal showing Jeff's not above the behavior he criticizes

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know, it's really disrespectful when students have their phones on in class. Shows a lack of respect for the learning environment.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I mean, come on, it's not that hard to turn it off for an hour.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: *phone buzzes* Oh, that's just... *checks phone frantically* ...that's important.

jokes.text: why would you not bring a pen to class? idiot. no, you're not an idiot
jokes.setup: Jeff needing a pen in class
jokes.punchline: His internal dialogue accidentally spoken aloud
jokes.explanation: Jeff's ego and social anxiety manifest in this awkward moment of thinking out loud

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Does anyone have a pen I could borrow?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Of course no one's going to lend me a pen. I'm too handsome. They're all intimidated by my confidence and my perfectly styled hair. Why would they help someone who clearly doesn't need help? I should probably just steal one from the professor's desk later.

jokes.text: maybe he didn't see you / he saw me! / maybe he had his blinker on too / maybe
jokes.setup: Shirley telling a parking lot story
jokes.punchline: Britta automatically taking the stranger's side and Shirley's frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for later bathroom conflict - Britta's contrarian nature vs Shirley's need for support

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: So this guy in the parking lot tells me I'm parked in his spot, and I say 'No, I've been here for twenty minutes.'

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Well, maybe he was circling looking for a spot the whole time.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Britta, he just got there!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You don't know that. Parking is stressful.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Why do you always do this?

jokes.text: [robotic voice] i am a female pleasure unit. i require a new coat of paint
jokes.setup: Discussion of makeovers and beauty standards
jokes.punchline: Britta's cynical robotic impression about femininity
jokes.explanation: Britta's feminist perspective delivered through absurd robot voice - perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I am a woman. I must wear makeup and heels to be valuable. Beep boop. My worth is determined by my appearance. Beep boop.

jokes.text: but of course you're saying, 'britta, aren't you a hypocrite? you're plastered with glitter and goo from head to toe, just like the rest of us.'
jokes.setup: Britta's anti-makeup rant
jokes.punchline: Her acknowledgment that she's heavily made up while criticizing makeup
jokes.explanation: Britta's self-awareness about her hypocrisy is both endearing and characteristic

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Makeup is just a patriarchal construct designed to make women feel insecure about their natural appearance. It's objectifying and completely unnecessary.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I would never wear that much makeup. It's ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: What? I'm not wearing makeup. This is just... my skin looks like this naturally.

jokes.text: the penis is a little small, i think
jokes.setup: Looking at Pierce's Vitruvian Man mascot design
jokes.punchline: Dean's awkwardly clinical assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean inappropriate observation delivered with uncomfortable casualness

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Well, the genitals are... anatomically correct. Which is either very brave or very, very stupid.

jokes.text: so...black?
jokes.setup: Dean explaining need for diversity in the mascot
jokes.punchline: Pierce's tone-deaf suggestion
jokes.explanation: Pierce completely missing the point about diversity - perfectly in character but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What about a Native American? They love mascots.

jokes.text: sorry to disturb you guys...who are clearly looking at a naked drawing of pierce doing jumping jacks
jokes.setup: Jeff walking in on Dean and Pierce with the mascot design
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact description of the bizarre situation
jokes.explanation: Jeff's deadpan delivery makes the absurd situation even funnier through understatement

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So... you two are designing a mascot together?

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Yes! We're creating something truly special for Greendale.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It's going to be magnificent!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I can see that. It's a guy with a giant head wearing a feather boa.

jokes.text: is that real bed head, or do you put something in there? can i? oh, it's crispy
jokes.setup: Pierce complimenting Jeff
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate hair touching and discovery
jokes.explanation: Pierce's boundary issues and Jeff's vanity about his hair combine well

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know Jeff, you have great hair. It's so silky.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Mind if I touch it?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Uh, yeah, actually I do mind.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Too late.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh my God! You're going bald!

jokes.text: jeffrey, did you not read the release agreement on the back of your registration form? i'm starting to see why someone used to be a lawyer
jokes.setup: Jeff complaining about promotional materials
jokes.punchline: Dean's passive-aggressive dig at Jeff's legal competence
jokes.explanation: Dean showing rare backbone against Jeff with a well-aimed insult

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Well Jeff, I'd offer you our legal team to review those materials, but I'm afraid they're all busy defending the college against your previous recommendations.

jokes.text: if you said, 'jump,' he'd say, 'how high?' if you said, 'stop,' he'd say, 'hammertime'
jokes.setup: Dean explaining Jeff's influence over Troy
jokes.punchline: The MC Hammer reference as an example of obedience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - mixing business speak with random pop culture in an endearing way

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Jeff has this strange influence over Troy. It's like he can make him do anything. Watch this. Troy, do the MC Hammer dance.

dialogue.speaker: Troy Barnes
dialogue.line: U can't touch this.

jokes.text: lawyer... companies? legal... gatherings?
jokes.setup: Dean trying to think of legal businesses
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to name law firms or legal organizations
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Dean's disconnect from the professional world. 'Legal gatherings' is wonderfully vague.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Okay, let me think of some legitimate law firms... uh... Legal... Eagles? No, that's a movie. How about... Law & Order? That's a TV show. Um... Attorneys at... Home? No, that's a store.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: What about... Justice League? No, that's superheroes. Okay, okay... The Bar Association? Wait, is that real or did I make that up?

jokes.text: i think so
jokes.setup: Jeff asking if Dean is trying to blackmail him
jokes.punchline: Dean's uncertain confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean uncertainty - he's not even sure he's blackmailing someone. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Are you trying to blackmail me?

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: I... I don't know. Am I?

jokes.text: that's a falcon with a gun
jokes.setup: Pierce presenting his mascot design
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt assessment of the ridiculous design
jokes.explanation: Jeff's matter-of-fact delivery makes Pierce's absurd design even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, that's a butt.

jokes.text: now it's a falcon with an erection
jokes.setup: Pierce's attempt to modify the design
jokes.punchline: Jeff's observation that it only got worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the previous joke - Pierce's 'improvements' only make things worse

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So Pierce, you tried to modify the design?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yeah, I made some improvements.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Improvements? It only got worse.

jokes.text: man, that backcountry today was epic! well worth the jellybeaning. blue bird day to shred some pow
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed hanging out
jokes.punchline: Their bizarre snowboarding slang conversation with made-up terms
jokes.explanation: Great nonsense dialogue that sounds authentic while being completely made up

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dude, did you see that gnarly shred session?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You mean the sick turfed rails? That was totally flibberflorp.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Flibberflorp? Bro, you can't just make up words.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Why not? You just said 'turfed rails.' That's not a thing either.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: It's... okay, fair point. But my made-up snowboarding term is cooler.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Is it though? Because I'm pretty sure I invented flibberflorp in my fever dream last week.

jokes.text: the dome arrives later. it comes with thunder
jokes.setup: Continuation of weird snowboard conversation
jokes.punchline: Abed's mystical-sounding but meaningless statement
jokes.explanation: Abed's ability to sound profound while saying nothing is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The snowboard is a vessel for the mountain's forgotten dreams, and only those who have seen the face of winter can truly understand the silence between the flakes.

jokes.text: you're saying i could be a lawyer / i'm saying you're a football player!
jokes.setup: Jeff's metaphor about ruling kingdoms
jokes.punchline: Troy's complete misunderstanding of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Troy's literal interpretation ruins Jeff's attempt at metaphor - good character work

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Life is like ruling a kingdom. You have to make tough decisions, navigate politics, and sometimes sacrifice your own desires for the greater good.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So you're saying I should get a throne?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, that's not—

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And a crown? Jeff, I'm gonna need a scepter.

jokes.text: annie said that benjamin button was compelling
jokes.setup: Jeff dismissing Annie's opinion about football
jokes.punchline: Using her movie taste as evidence she's wrong about everything
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture dig that reveals Jeff's snobbery and manipulation tactics

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, you think a football team should be named after a produce item. Your opinion is invalid.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's not fair!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You once told me 'Transformers 2' was a good movie. I don't need to hear anything else you have to say about anything ever again.

jokes.text: is that linebacker a pregnant woman?
jokes.setup: Troy looking at the football team
jokes.punchline: His observation about the team's quality
jokes.explanation: Shows the pathetic state of Greendale's team through Troy's innocent observation

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Those guys make a football team look like a group of guys standing around.

jokes.text: how did you know my nickname's t-bone? / because you're a football player, and your name begins with 't.' your name...begins...with 't.'
jokes.setup: Troy amazed Jeff knew his nickname
jokes.punchline: Jeff's obvious deduction delivered with dramatic emphasis
jokes.explanation: Jeff's condescending delivery and Troy's gullibility work well together

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, how did you know my nickname was 'The Truest Repairman'?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Troy... you introduce yourself that way. Every single time. To everyone.

jokes.text: i was just trying to throw you a bone because i like you / oh. you can keep that bone
jokes.setup: Britta explaining her bathroom offer
jokes.punchline: Shirley rejecting the patronizing gesture
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'bone' and shows Shirley's pride and intelligence

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Hey Shirley, you can use my bathroom anytime you need to. I know it might be hard for you.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Hard for me? Britta, I have a bathroom. I have several bathrooms. What I don't have is the patience for your condescending BS.

jokes.text: that is the ladies' room, britta, a place where ladies go to share, listen, support each other and... discreetly eliminate waste
jokes.setup: Shirley explaining bathroom etiquette
jokes.punchline: The clinical description mixed with the euphemism
jokes.explanation: Shirley's proper language contrasts nicely with the bathroom setting

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: And another thing - when you're in the bathroom, you need to courtesy flush. That means you flush while you're still going, so nobody has to hear your business.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: It's called being considerate. Number two shouldn't sound like a number two.

jokes.text: i've peed alone my whole life
jokes.setup: Shirley saying Britta needs to pee alone
jokes.punchline: Britta's tragic overstatement about her isolation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - turning bathroom habits into existential crisis with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Britta needs to pee alone.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm not allowed to pee with anyone around? What is this, solitary confinement? I'm basically in prison. I'm basically a prisoner of war. They're going to waterboard me next!

jokes.text: maybe it was when i got boobs before everyb--
jokes.setup: Annie wondering why women don't like her
jokes.punchline: Her overshare about early development getting cut off
jokes.explanation: Annie's tendency to overshare gets interrupted perfectly by social convention

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Why don't women like me? I'm fun, I'm smart, I'm a catch!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Maybe it's because you're always talking about yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's not true. Like, when I was in third grade, I developed early, and the other girls were really jealous of my—

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: See?

jokes.text: why are you doing a politically conservative high school shamefully outdated fight rap?
jokes.setup: Troy performing some kind of rap
jokes.punchline: Annie's overly analytical critique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie - over-analyzing everything with academic precision. 'Shamefully outdated' is great.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yo, yo, yo, check it out...

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Well, Troy, while your rhythmic cadence showed promise, your lyrical content lacked substantive thematic coherence. Your metaphors were derivative, and your use of internal rhyme schemes felt forced. Additionally, your breath control needs work—you rushed through the second verse, which compromised your articulation.

jokes.text: threw the skin around a couple of times. still got it. eh...eh...eh...eh... doosh!
jokes.setup: Troy explaining he played football
jokes.punchline: His crude slang and sound effects
jokes.explanation: Troy regressing to jock behavior - character appropriate but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I used to play football. I was pretty good too, until I got injured. Now I just make sound effects and crude slang about it.

jokes.text: what i need to know about the universe is that i'm at the center of it
jokes.setup: Troy dismissing astronomy class
jokes.punchline: His egocentric worldview
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's regression to jock arrogance while being ironically accurate given his protagonist status

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Astronomy? More like a-stro-NOMY. As in, it's not about me.

jokes.text: this isn't the real you. this is the arrogant jock that totally ignored the only people that truly liked him for non-superficial reasons but weren't allowed to say anything because their parents are bigots!
jokes.setup: Annie trying to talk Troy out of football
jokes.punchline: Her accidentally revealing too much about her high school experience
jokes.explanation: Annie's personal revelation disguised as general criticism - great character work and escalation

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Troy, you need to quit the football team. It's dangerous and you're too smart for this.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Annie, I like football. Why do you care so much?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Because I've seen what happens to star athletes. They peak in high school and then their lives fall apart. Trust me, I know all about high school.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What do you mean you know about high school?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I was a mathlete! I had a gun in my locker!

jokes.text: i think if we add in a little bit of phylicia rashad we'll be in better shape / yeah, you're right. that beige is a little light
jokes.setup: Designing a racially neutral mascot
jokes.punchline: Using a specific person as a color reference
jokes.explanation: Absurd approach to racial sensitivity by treating people as color swatches

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: We need a mascot that's racially neutral. How about we use Dean Pelton as a color reference?

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: I'm standing right here.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Exactly. Beige.

jokes.text: this is a chart of the features we're staying away from-- pan-asian eye folds, irish chins, women's breasts
jokes.setup: Explaining their approach to the mascot
jokes.punchline: The bizarre specificity of features to avoid
jokes.explanation: Pierce's clinical racism disguised as sensitivity - 'Irish chins' is particularly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: We need a mascot that doesn't have a beak, or talons, or feathers of any kind. And it can't be bipedal. Or have a tail. Or live in trees.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So what are we looking for here?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: A blob. A human-sized blob with no discernible features.

jokes.text: this is our human color wheel. it goes from seal to seal's teeth
jokes.setup: Showing their color chart
jokes.punchline: The extremely narrow range of 'human' colors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual joke about their attempts at racial neutrality resulting in blandness

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: This is our color chart showing the extremely narrow range of 'human' colors.

jokes.text: i think not being racist is the new racism
jokes.setup: Observing their mascot design process
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cynical take on performative anti-racism
jokes.explanation: Sharp, quotable line that captures the absurdity of their over-correction

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So we're designing a mascot that's offensive to no one, which means it'll be offensive to everyone. We're going to end up with a blue circle that apologizes for existing.

jokes.text: oh, yeah, a desmond tutu with just enough cream to make it a lou diamond phillips
jokes.setup: Pierce asking if Dean wants coffee
jokes.punchline: Dean's bizarre system of using people as coffee color references
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative and absurd extension of their color-coding system. Perfect Dean weirdness.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Want some coffee, Dean?

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Only if it's the color of Shirley's skin but darker, like if she got a spray tan from a bottle that was mostly iodine. Actually, no—the color of Troy's disappointment when he finds out we're out of those little chocolate chip cookies. Hmm, or maybe the shade of Jeff's soul. You know what? Just make it black.

jokes.text: maybe you should tell him you're hopelessly in love with him. high school must have been tough, huh?
jokes.setup: Jeff confronting Annie about Troy
jokes.punchline: His cruel but accurate psychological assessment
jokes.explanation: Jeff's lawyer skills turned cruel - perfectly in character but genuinely cutting

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You're in love with Troy. It's obvious. You light up around him, you find excuses to be near him, and you've completely reorganized your entire life around his schedule.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's not—I don't—

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And the worst part? He has no idea. Because you're so busy trying to be the perfect, put-together version of yourself that he'll actually notice, you've forgotten that people are attracted to authenticity, not performance.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wow. Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So either tell him how you feel, or move on. But stop pretending this is about study groups and friendship, because we both know it isn't.

jokes.text: you are just as selfish as i am. you're just not as good at it yet
jokes.setup: Jeff's analysis of Annie's motives
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment about her selfishness skills
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jeff's narcissism and his ability to wound with precision

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, your selflessness is really something. You're like a machine that converts oxygen into good intentions. It's actually kind of selfish how selfless you are—you're depriving the rest of us of the opportunity to be decent human beings.

jokes.text: profound, but technically meaningless
jokes.setup: Annie saying she cares
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dismissive analysis
jokes.explanation: Jeff's lawyer-speak used to deflect emotion - good character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, caring is just a gateway drug to disappointment. You start by caring about one thing, then it's two things, then suddenly you're crying at insurance commercials and voting.

jokes.text: but i'm here...to listen...girl
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to comfort Annie in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at feminine solidarity
jokes.explanation: Pierce's complete inability to understand appropriate behavior - 'girl' is perfectly awkward

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Annie, I know what you're going through. I'm a woman too, emotionally.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, that's not how that works.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: My mother was a woman. I think I understand the struggle.

jokes.text: but i'm here... to listen...girl.
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to be supportive in the women's bathroom
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at feminine solidarity
jokes.explanation: Pierce's uncomfortable attempt at female bonding. The awkward pauses and 'girl' make it cringey.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Ladies, I just want you to know that I'm here for you. We're all in this together.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I understand what it's like to menstruate. I have a very heavy flow, and I'm not ashamed to say it.

jokes.text: maybe...it's because men make the world go round?
jokes.setup: Annie asking why women inflict pain on themselves for men
jokes.punchline: Pierce's casually sexist response
jokes.explanation: Pierce's casual sexism delivered with complete sincerity - in character but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Because that's what women do. They suffer for love. It's beautiful, really.

jokes.text: there are guys out there that would kill to be with you. and if troy isn't one of them, that's him failing your standards
jokes.setup: Annie crying about Troy
jokes.punchline: Britta's surprisingly good advice
jokes.explanation: Britta being unexpectedly good at something - subverts expectations about her social skills

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Look, I know Troy means a lot to you, but you can't let yourself fall apart like this. You need to focus on what you can control right now.

jokes.text: 'scuse me! / i did it, i did it! / oh, yes, you did!
jokes.setup: Successful bathroom bonding session
jokes.punchline: Their celebration as they exit together
jokes.explanation: Sweet payoff to the bathroom storyline - simple but satisfying resolution

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: We did it! We actually bonded!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I know! This is amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: High five!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Yes!

jokes.text: i hurt myself on purpose / there was a scout coming to the final game at riverside and...i couldn't take the pressure, so i took the easy way out
jokes.setup: Troy explaining his injury
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it was self-sabotage
jokes.explanation: Major character revelation that recontextualizes Troy's entire arc - well-earned dramatic moment

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I was trying to open a jar of pickles, and I twisted my ankle.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Actually, I did it on purpose. I didn't want to go to Shirley's pot luck.

jokes.text: dude, we have feelings
jokes.setup: Troy insulting the team's abilities
jokes.punchline: Player's understated emotional response
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement - the team member's dignity in the face of insults

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You guys are terrible. I've seen better coordination at a DMV on a Monday morning.

dialogue.speaker: Football Player
dialogue.line: ...yeah, that hurt.

jokes.text: greendale, we're number one! nancy pelosi is no fun!
jokes.setup: Pep rally chant
jokes.punchline: Random political reference in school cheer
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur in the cheer - shows the school's general dysfunction

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: We are Greendale, hear us roar! We are Greendale, hear us roar!

jokes.text: it's not like greendale's gonna kill me or anything
jokes.setup: Jeff accepting his situation at Greendale
jokes.punchline: Famous last words before the mascot attacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the mascot chaos - classic 'famous last words' structure

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm actually starting to feel like this place isn't so bad. Maybe I can really make a difference here at Greendale.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What could possibly go wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Ahhhhh!

dialogue.speaker: Greendale Human Being Mascot
dialogue.line: *attacks*

jokes.text: the costume makes it difficult for him to see
jokes.setup: Mascot clearly stumbling around dangerously
jokes.punchline: Dean's casual explanation for obvious problems
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean understatement about an obvious safety hazard

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Oh, that's just our mascot working through some personal issues. He's fine.

jokes.text: he can't move his mouth either. but take it from me, that's human for 'hello.'
jokes.setup: Mascot's inability to speak
jokes.punchline: Dean's interpretation of nonverbal communication
jokes.explanation: Dean trying to make the best of a terrible mascot situation

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The mascot can't speak, but that doesn't mean he can't communicate. I've become quite fluent in his language of interpretive dance and awkward gestures.

jokes.text: we've solved racism. what's next? / uh, let's do something for little people. ooh! or albinos! / little albinos
jokes.setup: Their pride in the mascot design
jokes.punchline: Moving on to other groups to 'help' with increasing specificity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how their performative inclusivity will continue to new groups

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Our mascot design is really coming together.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: I'm so proud of what we've accomplished here.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: This is going to change everything for Greendale.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? We're so good at this, we should help other groups.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: That's a wonderful idea! We could assist the Drama Department.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: And then the pottery club needs our expertise.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Actually, I was thinking we'd help Derek specifically with his pottery wheel technique.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Or maybe just help Janet with her glazing method.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Why don't we just follow Marcus around and critique his every move?

tmdb_overview: Annie's dream of being the ex-football star's girlfriend is in jeopardy when Jeff is blackmailed into getting Troy to join Greendale's football team.
insight: Season 1's densest episode: 76 jokes in 39 minutes, yet cringe undercuts the laughs.
